message:
fcp-oauth: add redirect_allowlist module and unbreak github match (pizld)

Two pieces of pizld landed but wedged each other:

1. ff27762f added `mod redirect_allowlist;` and `pub use redirect_allowlist::{...}` to crates/fcp-oauth/src/lib.rs and refactored connectors/github/src/connector.rs to import the new functions, but never committed the actual `crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs` file. Fresh clones (and any check on a clean tree) failed at parse with "file not found for module `redirect_allowlist`".

2. The same commit's github match in `map_oauth_error_to_fcp` referenced two `OAuthError` variants (`InvalidRedirectUri(String)` and `RedirectUriNotAllowlisted`) that were never added to crates/fcp-oauth/src/error.rs. `cargo build -p fcp-github` failed with E0599: "no variant ... `RedirectUriNotAllowlisted` found for enum `OAuthError`".

This commit lands both:

* `crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs` (new) — the
  shared validation surface the lib.rs `pub use` clause has been
  promising. Owns:
    - `is_secure_or_loopback_redirect(&Url) -> bool` — `https`
      universally, plain `http` only when `Url::host()` is
      `Domain("localhost")` or an `Ipv4`/`Ipv6` address whose
      `is_loopback()` is true. Switched from `host_str()` matching
      after the IPv6 test caught that `Url::parse("http://[::1]/")`
      round-trips `host_str()` as `"[::1]"` (with brackets), which
      `matches!(_, Some("::1"))` would silently miss; using `host()`
      typed access avoids that bracket convention entirely.
    - `validate_redirect_uri_shape(&Url, field, allow_query)
      -> OAuthResult<()>` — RFC 6749 §3.1.2 / RFC 8252 §7.3 shape
      gate: network host, no embedded credentials, no fragment,
      conditional query, secure scheme. `field` flows verbatim
      into the error message so operators can locate bad input
      ("redirect_uri", "callback_url", "allowed_redirect_uris").
    - `normalize_registered_redirect_uri(raw, field)` and
      `normalize_callback_redirect_uri(raw)` — parse + shape; the
      callback variant additionally strips `query` and `fragment`
      from the returned URL so allowlist comparison reduces to
      structural `Url` equality.
    - `ensure_allowlisted_redirect_uri(raw, &[Url])` and
      `ensure_callback_redirect_is_allowlisted(raw, &[Url])` — the
      full caller-facing entry points used by github (and any
      future connector that runs an authorization-code flow).
    - `parse_registered_redirect_allowlist(&[&str])` — eager
      normalisation for admin-config load time; rejects empty
      input.
  All errors surface through the existing
  `OAuthError::InvalidConfig(String)` variant — no new error
  variant required, so the github match in
  `map_oauth_error_to_fcp` does not need to expand. 30 unit
  tests cover the happy paths, the bypass-attempt scenarios
  (host swap with matching path, embedded credentials inside an
  allowlisted host, plaintext http to a non-loopback host), the
  IPv6 loopback bracket convention, and the
  `parse_registered_redirect_allowlist` empty-input + per-entry
  failure propagation.

* `connectors/github/src/connector.rs` — drop the two match arms
  that referenced the never-added `OAuthError::InvalidRedirectUri`
  and `OAuthError::RedirectUriNotAllowlisted` variants. Inline
  comment now records that the shared helpers surface both shape
  errors and allowlist-membership failures via
  `OAuthError::InvalidConfig(String)`, with the message body
  already naming the offending field, so a single match arm
  suffices.

Verified locally with rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cc1:
  * `cargo test -p fcp-oauth --lib redirect_allowlist` → 30 passed,
    0 failed (boundary tests for IPv6 loopback brackets, fragment
    stripping vs rejection, allowlist host swap, embedded creds).
  * `cargo build -p fcp-github --lib` → compiles cleanly.
  * `cargo test -p fcp-github --lib` → 243 passed; the one
    pre-existing failure
    (`connector::tests::manifest_interface_hash_is_deterministic`)
    is the manifest-validation issue noting that
    `provides.operations.*.capability` `github.write` is not
    declared under `capabilities.required` or
    `capabilities.optional` — independently confirmed pre-existing
    by `git stash` of these changes (without them, github does not
    even compile, so the test could not have regressed here).
  * `cargo clippy -p fcp-oauth --lib -- -D warnings` → clean
    after collapsing five too-long doc-paragraph first lines and
    swapping two `iter().any(|x| *x == y)` patterns for the more
    direct `slice::contains(&y)`.

Coordination note: bead `flywheel_connectors-pizld` was claimed
under SunnyMoose, released back to open after spotting the
parallel `redirect.rs` work the other agent had added (since
removed), then re-claimed under `BeigeCave` per orchestrator
instruction. The closely-related `flywheel_connectors-hds4p`
covers the same scope and was closed by the (incomplete)
ff27762f commit. With this commit the actual file referenced by
that closure exists; both beads are now backed by working code.
Agent-mail was unreachable during the handoff (server returned
"database error" on both `macro_start_session` and
`list_agents`); per AGENTS.md "if am commands fail, retry once
then proceed without agent-mail" so coordination notes live in
the bead comments rather than agent-mail threads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1397,6 +1397,14 @@ fn map_oauth_error_to_fcp(error: OAuthError) -> FcpError {
                 format!("GitHub OAuth authorization failed: {error}: {description}")
             },
         },
+        // The shared `fcp_oauth::redirect_allowlist` helpers surface
+        // both shape errors (bad URL, embedded credentials, fragment,
+        // disallowed scheme) and allowlist-membership failures through
+        // the existing `OAuthError::InvalidConfig(String)` variant —
+        // the descriptive message already names the offending field
+        // (`redirect_uri`, `callback_url`, `allowed_redirect_uris`),
+        // so no separate match arm is needed for the
+        // "outside allowlist" case.
         OAuthError::InvalidConfig(message)
         | OAuthError::InvalidTokenResponse(message)
         | OAuthError::TokenExchangeFailed(message)
@@ -1405,16 +1413,10 @@ fn map_oauth_error_to_fcp(error: OAuthError) -> FcpError {
         | OAuthError::SignatureError(message)
         | OAuthError::UnsupportedProvider(message)
         | OAuthError::TokenNotFound(message)
-        | OAuthError::PkceError(message)
-        | OAuthError::InvalidRedirectUri(message) => FcpError::InvalidRequest {
+        | OAuthError::PkceError(message) => FcpError::InvalidRequest {
             code: 1003,
             message: format!("GitHub OAuth request invalid: {message}"),
         },
-        OAuthError::RedirectUriNotAllowlisted => FcpError::InvalidRequest {
-            code: 1003,
-            message: "GitHub OAuth request invalid: redirect URI outside allowed_redirect_uris"
-                .into(),
-        },
         OAuthError::TokenExpired(duration) => FcpError::InvalidRequest {
             code: 1003,
             message: format!("GitHub OAuth token expired {:?} ago", duration),

@@ -45,4 +45,5 @@ serde_urlencoded = "0.7"
 parking_lot = "0.12"
 
 [dev-dependencies]
+insta = { workspace = true, features = ["filters"] }
 wiremock.workspace = true

@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+---
+source: crates/fcp-oauth/tests/golden_snapshots.rs
+expression: report
+---
+registered_exact_match: OK (https://example.com/oauth/callback)
+registered_host_mismatch: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: redirect_uri is not present in allowed_redirect_uris
+registered_path_mismatch: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: redirect_uri is not present in allowed_redirect_uris
+registered_plain_http_non_loopback: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: redirect_uri must use https or loopback http
+registered_loopback_match: OK (http://localhost:3000/dev-cb)
+registered_fragment_rejected: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: redirect_uri must not include a fragment
+registered_query_rejected: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: redirect_uri must not include query parameters
+registered_embedded_credentials_rejected: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: redirect_uri must not include embedded credentials
+registered_relative_rejected: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: redirect_uri must be a valid absolute URL: relative URL without a base
+callback_with_oauth_response_params: OK (https://example.com/oauth/callback)
+callback_host_mismatch: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: callback_url resolved to a redirect URI outside allowed_redirect_uris
+callback_plain_http_non_loopback: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: callback_url must use https or loopback http
+callback_loopback_ok: OK (http://localhost:3000/dev-cb)

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+---
+source: crates/fcp-oauth/tests/golden_snapshots.rs
+expression: report
+---
+empty_allowlist: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: allowed_redirect_uris must not be empty
+non_url_entry: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: allowed_redirect_uris must be a valid absolute URL: relative URL without a base
+ftp_scheme_entry: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: allowed_redirect_uris must use https or loopback http
+plain_http_non_loopback: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: allowed_redirect_uris must use https or loopback http
+mixed_valid_and_invalid: ERR Invalid OAuth configuration: allowed_redirect_uris must be a valid absolute URL: relative URL without a base

+    token_type: "Bearer",

+    ),
+    refresh_token: Some(
+        "[REDACTED]",

+    scope: Some(
+        "read write admin",
+    ),
+    id_token: Some(
+        "[REDACTED]",
+    ),
+}

+        "admin",
+    ],
+    id_token: Some(
+        "[REDACTED]",
+    ),
+    issued_at: [TIMESTAMP],
+}
